title: manihot tripartita subsp. humilis
intro: Manihot tripartita subsp. humilis, also known as wild cassava or yuca, is a perennial plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its edible roots and tubers. The medicinal properties of Manihot tripartita subsp. humilis include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of consuming the plant's roots and tubers include improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function, making it a valuable resource for local communities. Manihot tripartita subsp. humilis is often prepared as a herbal tea, juice, or powder, and can also be used in traditional medicine preparations, such as infusions and decoctions.
